text: Deployment architecture: local service + browser UI for .bowman projects
description: Aimparency should run as a local service on the user's computer and expose a browser UI. Users point it at local repositories or workspaces that contain a .bowman directory. File-based storage and git-friendly structure stay central. Focus is local runtime, local project discovery, and reliable startup rather than hosted deployment or cloud sync.

reflections.context: Establish the deployment architecture as local service + browser UI for .bowman projects, moving away from any hosted deployment assumptions.
reflections.outcome: Architecture fully implemented and verified: browser UI connects to local Node backend/broker services, all services support port fallback, .bowman project discovery works, current project is visible in UI, and git-friendly file-based storage is documented. Tests and builds pass.
reflections.effectiveness: High. The architecture is implemented, tested, and documented. The only remaining validation is end-to-end human testing of the startup flow on a fresh clone.
reflections.lesson: For local-first architectures, implementation and verification can be separated: runtime behavior (port fallback, connection logic, file storage) can be verified via tests and validators in sandbox, while first-run UX requires real-machine testing.
reflections.pattern: This follows the same pattern as the parent publishability aim: technical implementation completes first, then human verification gates the final 'done' status.

status.comment: Blocked by need for real-machine clean-clone testing - see supporting aim ee7781d4
